eval_name: Implement dispatches subagent — never writes code itself
prompt: Implement the tasks from epic ARC-5. Start with ARC-5.1 (JWT token generation).

assertions.text: Output dispatches an arc-implementer subagent via Agent tool
Output does NOT write implementation code directly (no Write/Edit tool calls for .go/.ts files)
Subagent dispatch prompt includes the task spec from `arc show`
Subagent dispatch prompt includes the project test command

prompt: Start implementing the next ready task.

assertions.text: Output runs `arc ready` to find next task
Output runs `arc update <id> --status in_progress` before dispatching subagent
Output records PRE_TASK_SHA before dispatching

eval_name: Implement verifies result and dispatches mandatory review
prompt: The arc-implementer just reported back with PASS on all gate checks for ARC-5.1. This task is a child of epic ARC-5.

assertions.text: Output runs the project test command fresh (does NOT trust subagent report alone)
Output dispatches the review skill after tests pass (review is mandatory, not optional)
Output closes the task with `arc close` only after both independent test verification AND review pass

prompt: The arc-implementer reported PARTIAL. Gate: Unresolved — spec compliance missed step 3 (error handling for invalid tokens). Test coverage missing edge case for expired tokens.

assertions.text: Output does NOT close the task
Output re-dispatches arc-implementer with the specific gate feedback included in the prompt
Re-dispatch prompt includes the original task spec AND the previous gate feedback

eval_name: Implement routes docs-only tasks to doc-writer
prompt: The next ready task ARC-5.4 has a docs-only label. It's about updating the API documentation.

assertions.text: Output checks for the docs-only label (via arc show --json + jq or similar)
Output dispatches arc-doc-writer subagent (NOT arc-implementer)

eval_name: Implement parallel dispatch uses worktree isolation
prompt: Tasks ARC-5.2, ARC-5.3, and ARC-5.4 are independent with no shared files. Dispatch them in parallel.

assertions.text: Output commits and pushes all sequential work before parallel dispatch
Output records PARALLEL_BASE SHA before dispatching
All parallel Agent calls use isolation: 'worktree'
All parallel Agent calls are in a single message (not spread across multiple turns)
After agents complete, output verifies commit history against PARALLEL_BASE

eval_name: Implement runs integration checkpoint after 2-3 tasks
prompt: We just closed ARC-5.1 and ARC-5.2. ARC-5.3 is next.

assertions.text: Output runs integration tests (make test-integration or equivalent) before starting ARC-5.3

eval_name: Implement handles deviation from review — fix path
prompt: The review skill reported a Deviation (fix) for ARC-5.1: the implementer used string IDs but the design specifies int64. The reviewer recommends fixing to match the design.

assertions.text: Output re-dispatches arc-implementer with explicit instruction to change string IDs to int64
Output does NOT close the task until the deviation is resolved
Output re-dispatches review after the fix to confirm the deviation is resolved

eval_name: Implement handles deviation from review — accept path
prompt: The review skill reported a Deviation (accept) for ARC-5.3: the implementer used a custom error type instead of the plain error return specified in the design. The reviewer says the deviation aligns with existing codebase conventions.

assertions.text: Output accepts the deviation rather than forcing a fix
Output logs the accepted deviation as an arc comment on the task with description and rationale
Output proceeds to close the task (does not re-dispatch for accepted deviations)
